006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.client;
018
019import java.io.*;
020
021import org.apache.juneau.http.*;
022
023/**
024 * Converts a Java object passed to {@link RestRequest#body(Object)} into a {@link TransportBody}.
025 *
026 * <p>
027 * A list of converters is consulted in order; the first converter whose {@link #canConvert(Object)} returns
028 * {@code true} wins. The default converter list (applied automatically by {@link RestClient}) handles
029 * the common cases:
030 * <ol>
031 *    <li>{@link HttpBody} — passed through directly (streaming preferred over buffering)
032 *    <li>{@link InputStream} — wrapped in a streaming body
033 *    <li>{@code byte[]} — wrapped in a byte-array body
034 *    <li>{@link java.io.File} — wrapped in a file body
035 * </ol>
036 *
037 * <p>
038 * Custom converters are registered on the builder and are prepended to the default list
039 * (checked before the defaults). Use the {@link #of(Class, ThrowingConverter)} factory for concise lambdas:
040 *
041 * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
042 * <p class='bjava'>
043 *    RestClient <jv>client</jv> = RestClient.<jsm>builder</jsm>()
044 *       .bodyConverter(BodyConverter.<jsm>of</jsm>(MyBean.<jk>class</jk>,
045 *          <jv>bean</jv> -&gt; TransportBody.<jsm>of</jsm>(StringBody.<jsm>of</jsm>(<jv>mySerializer</jv>.serialize(<jv>bean</jv>)))))
046 *       .build();
047 * </p>
048 *
049 * <p>
050 * <b>Beta — API subject to change:</b> This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack
051 * ({@code org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*}).
052 * It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the <b>next major</b> Juneau release
053 * (and possibly earlier).
054 *
055 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
056 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/NextGenRestClient">juneau-ng REST client</a>
057 * </ul>
058 *
059 * @param <T> The type this converter handles.
060 * @since 9.2.1
061 */
062@SuppressWarnings({
063   "java:S2326" // 'T' is a phantom type parameter binding a converter to its handled type for type-safe registration (BodyConverter<?> lists) and the of() factory; removing it would break this public (beta) API.
064})
065public interface BodyConverter<T> {
066
067   /**
068    * Returns {@code true} if this converter can handle the given value.
069    *
070    * @param value The value passed to {@link RestRequest#body(Object)}. May be <jk>null</jk>.
071    * @return {@code true} if {@link #convert(Object)} should be called with this value.
072    */
073   boolean canConvert(Object value);
074
075   /**
076    * Converts the value to a {@link TransportBody}.
077    *
078    * @param value The value. Never <jk>null</jk> (converters are only called when {@link #canConvert} returns true).
079    * @return The transport body. Never <jk>null</jk>.
080    * @throws IOException If conversion fails.
081    */
082   TransportBody convert(Object value) throws IOException;
083
084   /**
085    * Creates a {@link BodyConverter} from a type predicate and a converting lambda.
086    *
087    * <p>
088    * The converter matches any value that is an instance of the given type.
089    *
090    * @param <T> The type to handle.
091    * @param type The type to match via {@code instanceof}. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
092    * @param converter The conversion function. Must not be <jk>null</jk>.
093    * @return A new converter. Never <jk>null</jk>.
094    */
095   static <T> BodyConverter<T> of(Class<T> type, ThrowingConverter<T> converter) {
096      return new BodyConverter<>() {
097         @Override public boolean canConvert(Object value) { return type.isInstance(value); }
098         @Override public TransportBody convert(Object value) throws IOException { return converter.apply(type.cast(value)); }
099      };
100   }
101
102   /**
103    * A checked function used by {@link BodyConverter#of(Class, ThrowingConverter)}.
104    *
105    * @param <T> The input type.
106    * @since 9.2.1
107    */
108   @FunctionalInterface
109   interface ThrowingConverter<T> {
110
111      /**
112       * Converts the given value to a {@link TransportBody}.
113       *
114       * @param value The value. Never <jk>null</jk>.
115       * @return The transport body. Never <jk>null</jk>.
116       * @throws IOException If conversion fails.
117       */
118      TransportBody apply(T value) throws IOException;
119   }
120}
